ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the performance metric */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value observed for the metric */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value observed for the metric */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of measurement for the metric */,
  "average_value" REAL /* Average value observed for the metric */,
  "standard_deviation" REAL /* Standard deviation of the observed values */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was first recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hardware_tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hardware test */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the hardware test */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date when the test was conducted */,
  "hardware_version" TEXT /* Version of the hardware being tested */,
  "tested_by" TEXT /* Name of the person who conducted the test */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of test (e.g., performance, stress, functional) */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test was conducted (e.g., lab, production, simulation) */,
  "test_setup" TEXT /* Detailed setup information for the test */,
  "test_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the test */,
  "test_status" TEXT /* Status of the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metric_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric result */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test associated with the result */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric associated with the result */,
  "observed_value" REAL /* Observed value for the metric during the test */,
  "test_time" TEXT /* Time at which the metric was observed */,
  "test_condition" TEXT /* Conditions under which the metric was observed */,
  "test_iteration" INTEGER /* Iteration number of the test */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the result was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metric_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES hardware_tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metric_results_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, tester, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test associated with the log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the log */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date of the log entry */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message detailing the log entry */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log entry (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the log (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the log entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_logs_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES hardware_tests ("test_id")
)
